location.office_time_required: up to 10% travel for onboarding, team meetings, industry events

level_signals: Title is 'Lead Product Manager' implying above Senior IC
Owns product strategy and roadmap for a major platform area
Comp range $168K-$252K base consistent with Lead/Staff PM level
Works with engineering team of 8-10 engineers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-7+ years of product management experience, ideally working on complex SaaS or workflow-based products
must_haves.qualifier: ideally complex SaaS or workflow-based products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building customer-facing product experiences with a strong UX focus
Demonstrated ability to simplify complex systems or workflows into intuitive user experiences
Strong product design instincts and experience partnering closely with design teams
Comfortable working closely with engineering on technically complex products
Experience owning core product surfaces with high usage and business impact
Highly customer-centric, with experience speaking directly with customers to inform product decisions
Strong communicator who can align cross-functional teams around product strategy and execution

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-7+ years of product management experience, ideally working on complex SaaS or workflow-based products
This is a highly customer-facing role where deep empathy for marketers and a strong design sensibility are essential.

scope.team_size_estimate: 8–10 engineers plus 1 co-PM in broader core product area
scope.surface: Orchestration experience — Flows, campaign editor, and workflow builder surfaces on the Klaviyo platform
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — ecommerce marketing automation platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of Flows and orchestration features
Retention and lifecycle marketing program effectiveness for customers
core_responsibilities: Own product strategy and roadmap for key orchestration experiences across flows, campaigns, and the Klaviyo editor
Lead the evolution of Flows, one of Klaviyo's most widely used automation capabilities
Simplify complex marketing workflows into intuitive, scalable product experiences
Partner closely with design to deliver thoughtful, high-quality UX for core product surfaces
Work with engineering to build reliable, scalable systems that support sophisticated automation use cases
Meet directly with customers (virtually and in-person) to gather feedback and deeply understand their workflow challenges
Define and track success metrics including adoption, engagement, and gross churn
Collaborate cross-functionally with data, support, sales, and marketing teams to ensure successful product adoption

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package; form not specified

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($168K–$252K base) and competitive for Lead PM
Clear product surface and ownership area (Flows / Orchestration — not vague)
Engineering team size explicitly stated (8–10 engineers)
Encourages close-but-not-exact-match applicants to still apply
Role impact clearly articulated — one of the most widely used surfaces in the product
red_flags: Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-US candidates
Wide compensation range ($84K spread) may indicate significant level variability or strong location-based adjustments
Location type is ambiguous — JD does not explicitly state hybrid/remote/onsite policy beyond travel requirement; cities not listed
Use of AI screening tool (Covey) disclosed — candidates in NYC should be aware of AEDT implications

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Lead PM', comp range, and responsibilities scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — ecommerce marketing automation SaaS)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; partial direct quote on adoption/engagement/gross churn)
location.type (inferred as hybrid based on travel requirement language; no explicit remote/onsite/hybrid policy stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'salary range reflects cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets' — implies US remote eligible)
ambiguities: Location type not explicitly stated as remote, hybrid, or onsite. JD mentions up to 10% travel and a broad US salary range, suggesting distributed/remote-friendly US role, but no explicit designation.

nice_to_have for 'SaaS or workflow-based products' overlaps with must-have years_pm_experience — the 'ideally' qualifier in the same sentence makes the domain preference soft while the years are hard.

Marketer domain knowledge ('deep empathy for marketers') is described as 'essential' in the JD but is listed under a descriptive sentence rather than the formal 'Who You Are' bullet list — treated as soft/nice-to-have given it reads as cultural context.
